description: Indulge in a classic American steakhouse experience right at home with this New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter recipe. Perfectly pan-seared to a tender, juicy finish, each bite is infused with a rich, aromatic garlic herb butter that elevates the natural beefy flavor. This dish is simple enough for a weeknight but elegant enough for a special occasion.

ingredients.notes: or other high smoke point oil

instructions.title: Prepare the Steaks
instructions.text: Remove the New York strip steaks from the refrigerator about 20-30 minutes before cooking to allow them to come closer to room temperature. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels. Season generously on all sides with coarse sea salt and freshly ground black pepper.

instructions.title: Heat the Skillet
instructions.text: Place a heavy-bottomed skillet, preferably cast iron, over high heat. Allow the skillet to heat until it is smoking lightly. Add the olive oil to the hot pan and swirl to coat the bottom.

instructions.title: Sear the Steaks
instructions.text: Carefully place the seasoned steaks into the hot skillet. Sear undisturbed for 3 minutes on one side to develop a deep, golden-brown crust. Flip the steaks and sear for another 3 minutes on the second side.

instructions.title: Add Garlic Butter and Baste
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium. Add the unsalted butter, smashed garlic cloves, fresh rosemary, and fresh thyme sprigs to the skillet. Once the butter is melted, tilt the pan slightly and, using a spoon, continuously baste the steaks with the aromatic garlic herb butter for 2-3 minutes.

instructions.title: Check Doneness
instructions.text: Continue to cook, flipping occasionally and basting, until the steaks reach your desired internal temperature. For medium-rare, aim for 130-135F (54-57C). For medium, aim for 135-140F (57-60C). Use an instant-read thermometer for accuracy.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked steaks to a cutting board. Loosely tent them with aluminum foil and let them rest for at least 10 minutes. This crucial step allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring a tender and moist steak. Slice against the grain and serve immediately, drizzling with any remaining pan drippings.

tags: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter
tips: For an authentic steakhouse crust, ensure your skillet is smoking hot before adding the steak. This creates a beautiful, caramelized exterior.
Use high-quality unsalted butter and fresh herbs for the best garlic butter flavor. Dried herbs can be used in a pinch, but fresh provides superior aroma and taste.
Always let your steak rest for at least 10 minutes after cooking. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and moist steak when sliced.
article:
Elevate your dinner game with a dish that’s both impressive and surprisingly simple to master: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter. This classic American dinner recipe transforms a premium cut of beef into a culinary masterpiece, bathed in aromatic garlic butter and herbs, delivering a restaurant-quality experience right in your own kitchen. Forget bland, dry steaks; this recipe focuses on developing a beautiful crust, a juicy interior, and an unforgettable flavor that will have everyone asking for seconds.

When it comes to iconic American comfort food, a perfectly cooked steak often tops the list. But what makes a steak truly exceptional? It’s not just the quality of the meat, but the technique and the thoughtful additions that enhance its natural richness. Here, we delve into the world of the New York Strip, and how a simple yet powerful combination of garlic and butter can turn an already great steak into an extraordinary one. Get ready to unlock the secrets to a steak that’s tender, flavorful, and incredibly satisfying.

## What is New York Strip Steak?

The New York Strip steak is a beloved cut of beef, famous for its excellent balance of tenderness, flavor, and texture. It comes from the short loin of the cow, a less-worked muscle, which contributes to its tenderness. Characterized by a fine grain and a good amount of marbling – those tiny flecks of fat that melt during cooking and infuse the meat with moisture and flavor – the New York Strip offers a robust, beefy taste that stands up well to bold seasonings and cooking methods.

Also known as the Kansas City Strip, Ambassador Steak, or simply "Strip Steak," this cut typically has a strip of fat along one edge, which can be trimmed or left on to render and add flavor. It’s boneless in most preparations, making it easy to cook evenly and carve. Its consistent shape and thickness also make it a favorite for searing in a hot pan or grilling, allowing for a beautiful crust to form while keeping the interior juicy and cooked to your desired doneness.

## Why You'll Love This Garlic Butter Steak Recipe

There are countless reasons why the New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter recipe will become a cherished staple in your home. Firstly, it delivers an unparalleled flavor experience. The rich, savory notes of the perfectly seared New York Strip are beautifully complemented by the aromatic, nutty goodness of melted butter infused with fresh garlic and herbs like rosemary and thyme. This creates a symphony of flavors that is both comforting and sophisticated.

Secondly, despite its gourmet appeal, this recipe is surprisingly easy to execute. With a few simple techniques and quality ingredients, you can achieve restaurant-quality results without the fuss. It’s an ideal dish for a special occasion, a romantic dinner, or even an elevated weeknight meal when you want to treat yourself. The interactive basting process not only ensures a moist and flavorful steak but also adds a touch of culinary theater to your cooking. Finally, the satisfaction of creating such a magnificent New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter from scratch is truly rewarding, making every bite taste even better.

## Ingredients for New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

To create the ultimate New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter, you'll need a handful of high-quality ingredients. The beauty of this dish lies in its simplicity, allowing each component to shine.

*   **New York Strip Steaks:** Two 1-1.5 inch thick boneless New York strip steaks, about 10-12 ounces each. Look for good marbling and a vibrant red color.
*   **Olive Oil:** 1-2 tablespoons, for searing. A high smoke point oil is ideal.
*   **Unsalted Butter:** 4-6 tablespoons (1/2 to 3/4 stick). Unsalted allows you to control the overall saltiness.
*   **Garlic:** 4-6 cloves, smashed and peeled. Fresh garlic is crucial for the best flavor.
*   **Fresh Herbs:** 2-3 sprigs of fresh rosemary and/or thyme. These infuse the butter with incredible aroma.
*   **Salt:** 1-2 teaspoons coarse sea salt or kosher salt. Don't be shy with the seasoning!
*   **Black Pepper:** 1/2-1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper.

Optional additions can include a pinch of red pepper flakes for a subtle kick, or a squeeze of lemon juice at the end to brighten the flavors.

## How to Make New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter (Step-by-Step)

Creating a sensational New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is a straightforward process that yields incredible results. Follow these steps for perfection:

1.  **Prepare the Steaks:** Remove steaks from the refrigerator 30-60 minutes before cooking to bring them closer to room temperature. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels – this is crucial for a good sear. Season generously all over with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
2.  **Heat the Skillet:** Place a heavy-bottomed skillet (cast iron is ideal) over high heat until it's smoking slightly. Add the olive oil and swirl to coat the bottom.
3.  **Sear the Steaks:** Carefully place the seasoned steaks into the hot skillet. Sear undisturbed for 2-3 minutes per side until a deep brown crust forms. If cooking more than two steaks, sear them one at a time to avoid overcrowding the pan and steaming the meat.
4.  **Add Butter, Garlic, and Herbs:** Reduce the heat to medium. Add the unsalted butter, smashed garlic cloves, and fresh herb sprigs to the skillet.
5.  **Baste:** As the butter melts, tilt the pan slightly and use a large spoon to continuously baste the melting garlic herb butter over the steaks. Continue basting for 2-4 minutes, or until the steaks reach your desired internal temperature. Use a meat thermometer for accuracy:
    *   Rare: 125°F (52°C)
    *   Medium-Rare: 130-135°F (54-57°C)
    *   Medium: 135-140°F (57-60°C)
    *   Medium-Well: 140-145°F (60-63°C)
6.  **Rest the Steaks:** Remove the steaks from the skillet and place them on a cutting board. Tent loosely with foil and let them rest for at least 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring a tender and moist result.
7.  **Serve:** Slice against the grain, if desired, and spoon any remaining garlic butter from the pan over the sliced steak before serving. Enjoy your perfectly cooked New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter!

## Expert Tips for Cooking the Best Steak

Achieving steakhouse-quality results at home is all about technique and attention to detail. Here are some expert tips to ensure your New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter turns out perfectly every time:

*   **Room Temperature is Key:** Never cook a steak straight from the fridge. Allowing it to come closer to room temperature ensures more even cooking from edge to center.
*   **Pat Dry, Pat Dry, Pat Dry:** Moisture on the surface of the steak prevents a good sear. Use paper towels to pat the steaks as dry as possible before seasoning.
*   **High Heat for Searing:** A screaming hot pan is essential for developing that coveted crispy, flavorful crust (Maillard reaction). Don't be afraid of a little smoke!
*   **Use a Cast Iron Skillet:** Cast iron retains heat exceptionally well and distributes it evenly, making it the ideal pan for searing steaks.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pan:** If cooking multiple steaks, work in batches. Overcrowding lowers the pan's temperature and steams the meat instead of searing it.
*   **Invest in a Meat Thermometer:** This is your best friend for achieving perfect doneness. Guessing can lead to overcooked or undercooked steak.
*   **Rest Your Steak:** This step is non-negotiable. Resting allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices, resulting in a more tender and moist steak. Skipping this step will result in juices running onto your plate instead of staying in the meat.
*   **Quality Ingredients Matter:** Start with a good quality New York Strip, fresh garlic, and good butter. These simple ingredients make a huge difference in the final flavor.
*   **Season Generously:** Steaks can handle a lot of seasoning. Don't be timid with salt and pepper; much of it will cook off or form the crust.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

The rich, savory flavors of New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter deserve equally delightful accompaniments. This versatile dish pairs beautifully with a variety of sides, making it perfect for any occasion.

For classic American comfort, consider serving your steak with creamy **mashed potatoes** or a decadent **baked potato** loaded with chives and sour cream. Roasted vegetables like **asparagus**, **broccoli**, or **Brussels sprouts** add a fresh, vibrant contrast and a touch of healthy goodness. A simple **garden salad** with a light vinaigrette can also cut through the richness of the steak, offering a refreshing counterpoint. For a more indulgent side, **creamed spinach** or **sautéed mushrooms** complement the beefy flavor perfectly.

When it comes to beverages, a robust **Cabernet Sauvignon** or a smooth **Merlot** are classic wine pairings that stand up to the steak's richness. If you prefer beer, a dark **stout** or a malty **amber ale** would be an excellent choice. Regardless of your choice, this New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is guaranteed to be the star of your dinner table.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is perfection, there's always room for creative variations to suit your taste or what you have on hand.

*   **Herbs:** Don't limit yourself to rosemary and thyme. Fresh sage, oregano, or even a sprinkle of chopped fresh parsley added at the very end can introduce different aromatic notes.
*   **Garlic:** For a milder garlic flavor, you can use roasted garlic cloves instead of fresh, or a touch of garlic powder in the seasoning rub (though fresh is always best for the butter basting).
*   **Spices:** Enhance your steak's crust by adding a pinch of paprika, onion powder, or even a tiny dash of cayenne pepper to your salt and pepper rub.
*   **Different Cuts of Steak:** While the New York Strip is fantastic, this garlic butter method works wonderfully with other cuts like ribeye, sirloin, or even a thicker flank steak (adjust cooking times accordingly).
*   **Cooking Method:** If you prefer grilling, sear the steak over high heat on the grill, then move it to a cooler zone and brush with the melted garlic butter mixture during the last few minutes of cooking. For a reverse sear, cook low and slow in the oven first, then finish with a quick, hot sear in the skillet with the garlic butter.
*   **Compound Butter:** Prepare the garlic herb butter in advance by mixing softened butter with minced garlic and herbs, then rolling it into a log and chilling. Slice off pats of butter to add to the pan during the basting phase.

## Frequently Asked Questions About New York Strip Steak

**Q: What is the best way to cook a New York Strip steak?**
A: Pan-searing in a hot cast-iron skillet, followed by basting with garlic herb butter, is widely considered one of the best methods for achieving a perfect crust and juicy interior. Grilling is also an excellent option.

**Q: How long should I cook a New York Strip steak for medium-rare?**
A: For a 1 to 1.5-inch thick New York Strip, typically 3-4 minutes per side for the initial sear, followed by 2-4 minutes of basting, will yield medium-rare (internal temperature of 130-135°F / 54-57°C after resting). Always use a meat thermometer for accuracy.

**Q: Do I need to rest my steak after cooking?**
A: Absolutely! Resting your steak for 5-10 minutes after cooking allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, resulting in a more tender, flavorful, and moist steak. Skipping this step can lead to a dry steak.

**Q: Can I use a bone-in New York Strip steak for this recipe?**
A: Yes, bone-in New York Strips are fantastic. The bone adds flavor and helps the steak cook more evenly. Just be aware that bone-in steaks may take a few minutes longer to cook to your desired doneness.

**Q: What's the difference between a New York Strip and a Ribeye?**
A: Both are premium cuts, but they have distinct differences. Ribeye steaks are known for their abundant marbling and rich, buttery flavor, often considered fattier and more tender. New York Strip steaks are leaner than ribeyes but still very tender, offering a robust, beefy flavor with a slightly firmer texture and a tighter grain.
